analysis.problem: Groundwater depletion in Florida due to public use and agricultural activities
analysis.root_cause: Over-extraction of groundwater for households, municipalities, businesses, and agriculture, exceeding the natural recharge rate
leverage_points: Government regulations and water management policies
Agricultural practices and water efficiency measures

proposed_actions.action: Implement water-saving technologies and practices in agriculture
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Reduce groundwater extraction by 20% within the next 5 years

proposed_actions.action: Develop and implement a comprehensive water conservation plan for households and municipalities
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Reduce per-capita water consumption by 15% within the next 3 years

evidence: Public use by households, municipalities, and businesses accounts for the largest depletion of groundwater in Florida
Agriculture is responsible for at least a quarter of groundwater withdrawals in Florida
analysis.generalization: This problem connects to the domains of Civilization (water management and resource allocation) and Planet (ecosystem health and biodiversity)
analysis.summary: Groundwater depletion in Florida poses a significant threat to the state's water security, and addressing this issue requires a multi-faceted approach that involves government regulations, agricultural practices, and water conservation efforts.

raw_items.title: Why Florida is running out of water | Grist
raw_items.summary: Jan 8, 2025 · Public use by households, municipalities, and businesses accounts for the largest depletion of groundwater in Florida, while agriculture is responsible for at least a quarter of withdrawals.

raw_items.title: People are flocking to Florida. Will there be enough water ...
raw_items.summary: Jan 13, 2025 · Compared to places where groundwater is already severely depleted, like California, Mexico and Arizona, Florida has the luxury of one of the highest-producing aquifers in the world, and more time to prepare for a dearth of supply.

raw_items.title: Why some regions are winning the fight against groundwater depletion
raw_items.summary: Declining water tables leave entire regions vulnerable to drought, land subsidence or seawater intrusion while damaging ecosystems and reducing water access. Properly securing this resource is a matter of social, humanitarian and environmental security.

raw_items.title: Groundwater depletion is a looming crisis, Michigan needs proactive...
raw_items.summary: We should have addressed this crisis sooner. Neglecting gradual groundwater depletion was a mistake. In 2020, farmers and livestock operations in Michigan used 187 billion gallons of water, double the amount in 2009.
